name: Brazilian Iced Chocolate Coca-Cola
recipeInstructions:
1. In the top of a double-boiler over hot water, melt the chocolate squares.
2. Stir in the sugar.
3. Gradually stir in the hot coffee, mixing thoroughly.
4. Add the milk and continue cooking until all particles of the chocolate are dissolved and the mixture is smooth, about 10 minutes.
5. Pour into a jar and cover. Chill.
6. When ready to serve, stir in the chilled Coca-Cola over ice cubes in tall glasses.
7. For a beverage, top with whipped cream.
8. For a dessert, add a scoop of vanilla ice cream.
Makes 5 cups.
Recipe: "International Cooking with Coca-Cola", a give-away pamphlet from The Coca-Cola Company, 1981
